prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, controlled pacing with deliberate cuts and extended holds.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. atmosphere of a weight finally laid down; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
a kneeling figure at the foot of a rough cross, a heavy travelling burden set down on the ground beside loosened open hands, the shoulders eased, the head bowed in relief, warm light falling on the released load and the bare feet, deep shadow beyond, the weight laid down with nothing more to bear. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — the whole painted scene.

Cut to mid framing — the dominant subject of the painting.

Cut to close-up — the set-down burden.

Cut to insert — the loosened empty hands.

Cut to macro detail — the bare feet.

Cut to lower framing — the rough foot of the cross.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the set-down burden.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
a weight finally laid down

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image

beats.description: Start on full composition — the whole painted scene.
Cut to mid framing — the dominant subject of the painting.
Cut to close-up — the set-down burden.
Cut to insert — the loosened empty hands.
Cut to macro detail — the bare feet.
Cut to lower framing — the rough foot of the cross.
End hold on the set-down burden.
